name: German A2 Vocabulary
description: Elementary German — CEFR A2 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: aber — again. Also: rather; quite; unusually; used with adjectives to express a surprising degree, whether this surprise be real or for effect, nonetheless, nevertheless
Abitur — final exams taken by pupils at the end of their secondary education in Germany and Finland to attain eligibility for all kinds of universities (allgemeine Hochschulreife)..
Abschied — parting, farewell.
Abschluss — exclusion. Also: end (finish), graduation (the act or process of graduating)
Abteilung — department. Also: division, battalion
Adjektiv — adjective.
Adventskranz — Advent wreath.
Agentur — agency.
Aktion — operation, campaign, measure, organized course of action. Also: sales campaign, act, deed, typically unusual, often negative, action, activity, action, impulse
Allergie — allergy.
Alltag — everyday life, daily routine.
Alter — age, old age. Also: antiquity, epoch, age

Amt — agency; department; office (state institution responsible for specified concerns). Also: office; post (public function, e.g. of a civil servant), mass; office, administrative unit (of a country); district

Angabe — verbal noun of angeben. Also: indication, specification, declaration (providing of information), statement, the provided information itself, service, synonym of Angeberei (“the act of bragging, boasting”), information attached to a verb
Anhang — addendum. Also: supplement, attachment (e.g., of an email), appendage, appendix, entourage (A retinue of attendants, associates or followers)
Anlage — public green area, site, park, garden. Also: attachment of a letter, document or email,, plant, facility, installation, device, system, rig, music centre, annex, appendix, asset, disposition
Anmeldung — application. Also: announcement, booking, registration, login, logon, reporting, place where one signs up or registers
Antrag — application, request. Also: motion, proposition, petition, proposal of marriage
Apfelbaum — apple tree.
Arbeitgeber — employer (male or of unspecified gender).
Arbeitsstelle — job, position. Also: roadworks
Art — kind, sort, type. Also: species, nature, character, behaviour, way, method, mode, mood, voice
Ast — bough, limb, branch.

auch — also, too, as well (in addition to whatever or whoever has previously been listed). Also: Used for emphasis., To confirm a preceding statement by someone else: really, actually, indeed, in fact, To ask for confirmation of something one considers necessary., To imply that something is unreasonable., even (implying an extreme example in the case mentioned, as compared to the implied reality), ever as in whatever, whenever, however, etc.
Aufenthalt — stay; residence; presence (the state of being in a place). Also: sojourn; stop (a short stay), residence (a place where one lives or stays)
Aufregung — excitement. Also: agitation
Aussage — proposition. Also: statement, claim
aussehen — to look, seem. Also: [with nach (+ dative) ‘like someone/something’], to look good or bad; depending on intonation, to look out, to watch out, to look for and find; to pick; to select
Ausstellung — exhibition.
Auswahl — selection. Also: choice, range, assortment, digest, reader, anthology, national sports team
Autofahrer — A motorist (the driver of a car or automobile)..
Autor — author.
Autounfall — car accident, car crash.
Band — tape, ribbon. Also: A ligament, band or tie holding items together, belt (conveyor belt, fan belt, etc.), band of the spectrum, intimate bond to a person, dependence, social bond, shackle

Bau — building, construction (the act of constructing). Also: building site, construction site (place where such constructing takes place), building, construction, edifice (built-up structure, house), burrow (of an animal, especially underground), prison, jail
Bauernhaus — farmhouse (farmer's residence).
Baumarkt — large hardware store, typically run by a chain, often including a garden and/or furniture department.
Baustelle — building site, construction site. Also: matter, unfinished work, area that needs to be addressed
Becher — a cylindrical or slightly conical drinking vessel without a stem, typically with no handle, beaker.
Bedienung — service. Also: waiter, waitress, operation
Beet — bed (for plants).
Beginn — start, beginning.
Behinderung — handicap, disability. Also: hindrance, impediment, obstacle
Beratung — discussion. Also: consultation, advice
Bereich — area, realm, range, scope. Also: course of study or domain of knowledge or practice; field, region
Beste — best (female person).

Betrieb — operating or running a technical device, application or process, in other words planful activity under the exploitation of tools, connections and/or manpower with an inherent direction towards an economic result, industry, operation, verbal noun of betreiben. Also: the organizational unit of a company wherewith the entrepreneur pursues a work-technical purpose thus, works
Bevölkerung — population.
Beziehung — relation, relationship, connection. Also: interpersonal relationship, terms, relations, romantic relationship
Bilderbuch — picture book.

Blatt — leaf (green and flat organ of a plant). Also: petal (one of the parts of the whorl of a flower), sheet, piece of paper (piece of paper (usually rectangular) that has not been cut, torn, or folded), sheet (full sheet of paper in a newspaper or similar periodical, representing four pages), hand, cards (set of cards held by a player), paper, newspaper (publication containing news and other articles), magazine (a printed and published informative periodical), (scientific) journal, blade (flat functional end of an oar, rudder, propeller, etc.), blade (sharp cutting edge of a saw or other tool), thin plate; foil (very thin sheet of metal)
Blumenstrauß — bouquet (bunch of flowers).

Boden — ground, soil. Also: sea bottom (typically called Meeresboden), any defined type of soil, floor, attic, garret, loft, flooring, floor cover (often used in this sense in compound nouns: Teppichboden, Parkettboden)
Bohne — bean.
Bohrmaschine — drill (tool).
Boot — boat.
Brauch — custom.
Brautpaar — bridal couple.
Brett — board, plank. Also: energetic, forceful track
Briefkasten — mailbox, letterbox.
Briefmarke — postage stamp.
Briefumschlag — envelope (for a letter).
Buchhandlung — bookstore, bookshop.
Buchmesse — book fair, the most famous one in the world being the annual, international Frankfurter Buchmesse at Frankfurt.
Buchstabe — letter (of the alphabet). Also: letter (a division unit of a piece of law marked by letters)
Bundesland — federal state (non-sovereign state). Also: One of the 16 federal states of Germany, One of the 9 federal states of Austria
Bundesrepublik — federal republic.

Bürger — citizen, inhabitant, resident; legally recognized inhabitant of a city or state. Also: bourgeois, burgher, member of the middle class, burgher, freeman, usually richer and/or more influential than a peasant/worker but poorer and/or less influential than a nobleman
Bürgermeister — mayor (leader of a city, male or unspecified sex).
Chor — choir; chorus (group of people singing together). Also: choir (part of a church building)
Dach — roof. Also: circumflex, head, especially the scalp, a rock layer above a seam
Dauer — duration. Also: permanence
Dauerfrost — permafrost.
Decke — any cloth or cushion used as a covering; a tablecloth, blanket, quilt, duvet, etc.. Also: ceiling (surface at the upper limit of a room or cavity)
Demokratie — democracy.
Dieb — thief (male or of unspecified gender).
Diskussion — discussion, debate.
Download — a download.
dritte — third.
dunkeln — to darken.
Ehe — marriage (state of being married; life as a married couple).
Ehefrau — married woman, wife.
Eindruck — impression.
Einfamilienhaus — family house, family home (house designed to hold a single family).
Einfluss — inflow, influx. Also: influence
Einführung — introduction.
Einkauf — purchase (the act or process of seeking and obtaining something). Also: whole set of purchased goods
einkaufen — to shop. Also: to buy, to purchase
Einkaufstüte — shopping bag.
Einkaufszentrum — shopping center, shopping mall.
Einrichtung — facility, institution. Also: constitution, installation, establishment, furnishing, furniture
Eintrittskarte — admission ticket.
Elternzeit — parental leave.
Engagement — commitment, dedication. Also: booking (of an artist etc.), engagement
Enkel — grandchild. Also: grandson
Entgelt — charge, paid money.
Entscheidung — decision.
Entwicklung — evolution. Also: development
Ereignis — event (occurrence of social or personal importance).
Erfahrung — experience (event through which empirical knowledge is gained).
Erholung — recovery, convalescence. Also: recreation
Erinnerung — memory (recall); remembrance. Also: reminder, something that reminds, memento, the objects wherewith the memory is supported, a kind of legal recourse in civil procedure
Erlebnis — experience (event of which one is cognizant).
Ermäßigung — discount.
Ernährung — nutrition.
Erziehung — education; upbringing; character formation (process of imparting basic knowledge, judgment, morals, and manners, especially to a child). Also: training (of a pet)
Essgewohnheit — eating habit.
etwas — somewhat, slightly. Also: a little, a bit
Fahrzeug — vehicle. Also: motor vehicle, automobile, car
Faktum — fact (something concrete used as a basis for further interpretation).
Familienmitglied — a family member.
Feiertag — holiday (a day on which a festival, etc., is traditionally observed).

Feld — field (plot of open land, especially one used to grow crops). Also: area where action, often competitional, takes place, field, battlefield, field, playing field, field, practical observation, field, a domain of work, knowledge, study, a defined area where something is put in, where information is supplied, field, background, array, square
Fernsehprogramm — television program. Also: television channel
Feuer — fire (chemical process). Also: fire (instance of something burning), fire; the act of firing, a means for lighting a fire, i.e. a lighter or matches
Filiale — branch (office of an organization with several locations).
Filmemacher — filmmaker.
Flecken — Fleky (a municipality of the Czech Republic).
Folge — sequence. Also: consequence, episode
Fortbildung — further education, continuing education.
Fotografin — female photographer.
Freitagnachmittag — Friday afternoon.
Freundschaft — friendship. Also: relations, relatives
Friedhof — cemetery, graveyard.
Frisur — haircut (way hair is cut). Also: hairdo; hairstyle (way hair is arranged)
Fundbüro — lost and found, lost property, lost articles.
Fußballspiel — football, soccer. Also: football match, soccer game
Fußgänger — pedestrian, walker (male or of unspecified gender).
Föhn — foehn (warm wind which appears on the leeward side of mountains, for example on the northern side of the alps in south Germany). Also: hair dryer
Führung — lead. Also: leadership, command, management, conducted tour, conduct
Galerie — gallery (all senses).
Garantie — guarantee. Also: warranty
Gasthaus — restaurant, inn, guesthouse.
Gaststätte — restaurant.
Geburt — birth.
Gebäude — building, edifice, structure.
Gedanke — thought. Also: idea, plan, concept
Gedicht — poem.
Gefühl — feeling (either physical sensation or emotion). Also: sense of something, instinct
Gegend — area, region.
Gegenteil — opposite (contrary thing).
Gehalt — content, fraction (amount or percentage of some material in a mass). Also: content (that which is inside, contained)
Geldbörse — wallet, purse.
Gelegenheit — opportunity, chance, instance, occasion. Also: facility (facilities), accommodation
Geruch — smell, odour. Also: sense of smell, olfaction, (figurative, dated) reputation
Gerät — device, tool, appliance, machine. Also: equipment, tools, contents, furniture
Gesamtschule — comprehensive school.
Geschmack — taste. Also: flavour, smell, odor

Gesellschaft — company (presence of other people in general). Also: company (group of people), society, the public (abstract concept of an organised social group or a local population in general), society (organised group of people with common interests), company, corporation (collection of people and assets with a legal identity, which may or may not be separate from that of its members)

Gesetz — law, legal act, bill, statute. Also: a bill by the parliamentary legislator according to the procedures devised in the state’s constitution, any formally published legal norm, a regulation affecting anyone in an unlimited number of cases, a principle governing the phenomena in a particular field of science, a law of nature, ellipsis of Gesetzessammlung (“lawbook”)
Gesicht — face. Also: a facial expression, a look, that which is outward or in front, honour, reputation, person, individual, sight, vision (the ability to see)
Golfplatz — golf course.
Grab — grave. Also: tomb
Grenzübergang — border crossing.
Grippe — flu; influenza; grippe..
Grund — ground, land (usually as someone's property). Also: bottom (of a container or a body of water), reason; motive, cause
Grundschule — elementary school, primary school.
Grundstück — land, parcel, real estate.
Grüne — greenness.
Gutschein — something that attests (monetary) value, particularly a voucher, gift certificate, coupon, or rain check.
Gymnasium — grammar school (UK), prep school (US) (school used to prepare students for university).
Handtasche — handbag, (US) purse.
Handtuch — towel (cloth for wiping).
Hauptrolle — lead (starring role in a drama).
Hauptschüler — Hauptschule student.
Hausschlüssel — housekey.
Haustier — A domestic animal, in the senses of livestock and beast of burden; also called Nutztier.. Also: A pet; sometimes called Heimtier.
Heiligabend — Christmas Eve (day before Christmas Day).
Heimweh — homesickness.
Heirat — marrying; marriage; wedding (the act of marrying; the contracting of a marriage).
Helm — helmet. Also: helm roof, helmet, as shown above a coat of arms
Hintergrund — background. Also: context, background information (information about history, motivation or the wider situation surrounding something)
Hitze — heat.
hoch — high, tall. Also: high, great, immense, grand, important, hard to comprehend, confusing

Hochzeit — wedding, marriage (a ceremony in which people are married and/or a celebration thereof). Also: the state of having been dealt both queens of clubs; used in a player’s announcement that they were dealt these and that the first player to win a non-trump trick will join their side
Hofburg — the principal residence of the Habsburg dynastic rulers, located in Vienna. Also: present-day residence of the president of Austria
Hoffnung — hope, the belief or expectation that something wished for can or will really happen.. Also: hope, the actual thing wished for, hope, a person or thing that is a source of hope
Holz — wood (material). Also: a particular kind of wood, a piece of wood, usually small, grove; woods; small forest
Honorar — honorarium. Also: royalty payment
Hälfte — half; fifty percent of something. Also: half, share; any of two correlating pieces, whatever the size, middle; the virtual line where two equal halves meet
Höhepunkt — highlight; climax; high; high point. Also: orgasm, climax
Hüfte — hip; hips.

Identität — sameness, identicalness, identity. Also: recurring characteristics that enable recognition, sense of what someone or something is, identity, An identification sign or mask behind one which someone hides and may or may not enable recognition, identity, unity
Inhalt — content.
Jahreszeit — season, time of year.
Jahrhundert — a century (period of 100 years).
Kabel — cable (electronic wire). Also: telegram, thick rope
Kaffeemaschine — coffeemaker.
Kaiser — emperor (ruler of certain monarchies; highest monarch).
Kaiserin — empress (wife or widow of an emperor).
Kandidat — candidate.
Karotte — carrot.
Karton — carton; cardboard box (e.g. a moving box). Also: cardboard, paperboard, cartoon (preliminary sketch)
Kassette — cassette. Also: coffer
Keller — cellar. Also: basement
Kindheit — childhood.
Klavierunterricht — piano lesson.
Knopf — button (fastener). Also: button (mechanical device), stud, knob, e.g. a doorknob, a simple knot
Knödel — dumpling.
Kommode — chest of drawers.
Kompliment — compliment.
Konditor — confectioner, pastry cook (male or of unspecified gender).
Konflikt — conflict.
Konkurrenz — competition (act of competing). Also: competitors, competition (rival businesses)
Konsulat — consulate.
Kontrolle — inspection. Also: control
Konzern — business group, corporate group.
Kopierer — copier (a machine that copies).
Kredit — loan or anything afforded to someone in the expectation of return. Also: credibility, informal credit rating, the ability of someone to get loans based on his reputation
Krimi — crime story, crime thriller. Also: police procedural, an exciting event, a course of events whose expected outcome changes often
Krimiserie — procedural, crime series.
Kritik — criticism (negative judgement). Also: criticism, review (general judgement), critique, review (review of a work of art)
Kutsche — carriage, coach (a wagon, usually upholstered, pulled by horses). Also: a large automobile, especially an old-fashioned one
Käfig — cage.
Kälte — coldness; cold.
Köchin — cook, a cooker (female).
Küchentisch — kitchen table.

laden — to load (something) e.g. into a container or onto a vehicle, to load up. Also: to load (some weapon), to load (some data) from a store, to download from a network, to charge (a battery or capacitor) with electricity
Lastwagen — truck, lorry.
Laune — mood, humor. Also: whim
Lebenslauf — curriculum vitae (UK), résumé (US).
Lederjacke — leather jacket.
Lehre — teaching, tenet. Also: apprenticeship, doctrine, lesson, theory (system of knowledge related to one aspect of a field of study), science, jig, gauge (tool)
Lehrstelle — apprenticeship position.
Leidenschaft — passion (powerful emotion). Also: passion (desire, often sexual), passion (fervent interest in something), the object of such interest, e.g. a hobby
Lenkrad — steering wheel.
Lenkung — steering.
Leserin — female reader.
Lesung — reading (incl. poetry, prose, legislative, and liturgical). Also: reading, lection, lesson
Liebesfilm — a romance film.
Lieblingsbuch — favorite book.
Limonade — lemonade. Also: any carbonated soft drink; soda; pop; fizzy drink (particularly kinds other than cola, though it may also be included)
Limousine — saloon car/sedan (car with a lowered boot/trunk). Also: limousine (luxury version of the above)
Liste — list.
Liter — liter, litre.

Literatur — literature (written works collectively, often about a certain topic, in a certain language etc.). Also: semantic narrowings in various contexts, the works consulted by an author, especially a scientist, as resources for a given text, written fiction and poetry, especially that deemed of high cultural value

Loch — hole; perforation. Also: hole in the ground; pit, gap; bare spot, cavity, dungeon; underground prison, prison; jail, apartment, flat or house in a bad condition; dump, boring small town or village
Lohn — wage. Also: reward
Lokal — A restaurant, especially when small and/or traditional; a pub that serves hot food; an inn.. Also: A room or facility used for public services.
Lärm — annoying noise; noise (when subjectively considered to be a disturbance).
Macht — power, authority. Also: might, strength
Magen — stomach, maw.

Medium — media, medium (format for communicating or presenting information). Also: medium (the nature of the surrounding environment, e.g. solid, liquid, gas, vacuum or a specific substance), medium (someone who supposedly conveys information from the spirit world), middle voice
Medizin — medicine (the study of disease and treatment; the profession based thereon). Also: medicine (healing substance or cure)
Medizinstudium — medicine studies, medical education.
Meeresluft — sea air, maritime air.
Meisterschaft — mastery. Also: championship
Menge — quantity. Also: multitude, crowd, gathering of people, set
Messe — fair (trade exhibition). Also: mass, the eucharistic liturgy
Metall — metal.
Methode — manner. Also: approach, technique, method (process by which a task is completed), method (subroutine or function in object-oriented languages)
Metropole — metropolis; very large city; city which dominates its surroundings due to its population, administrative power or culture(s).
Metzgerei — butcher's, butcher's shop.
Mieter — renter. Also: tenant
missfallen — to displease, to dissatisfy.
miteinander — together (with). Also: with each other
Mitglied — member.
Mitschüler — schoolmate (person one attends or attended school with). Also: classmate (person who is or was in one's class/form)
Mitteilung — message (communications: Information which is sent from a source to a receiver).
Mittelalter — dark ages, an uncultured or barbaric era (from a misrepresented view with roots in Renaissance and Protestant thought). Also: middle age (of life), middle-aged people
Mittelpunkt — centre. Also: centre of attention, interpunct
Motor — engine (mechanical device).
Mountainbike — mountain bike.
Märchen — fairy tale (folk tale). Also: fairy tale (unbelievable story)
Möbelstück — piece of furniture (an item that belongs to the furniture of a room).
Mülltonne — wheelie bin; garbage can (large container for wet rubbish, usually outdoors and now usually on wheels).
Nachteil — disadvantage, demerit. Also: drawback
Nachtisch — dessert, pudding (UK).
Nebel — fog, mist, haze. Also: nebula
Nebensatz — subordinate clause. Also: aside (statement made in passing)

nehmen — to take (something into one's possession or on one's body). Also: to take from, to hold (in one's hands), to grasp, to take, to consider (a statement, a situation, an idea, etc, in a certain way, for example seriously, badly, personally), to capture, to arrest, to ingest (e.g. a pill), to move into, to sit at (one's assigned position), to use (time, effort, etc. for a specific purpose), To begin or cause the action implied by a noun to take place, possibly making the sentence more passive or indirect., to cause oneself to be (in some state); to become; to take oneself (to some state), to seize, to capture, to receive, to accept, to foul

Niveau — level (the physical elevation at which something is located or constructed). Also: level (the approximate magnitude of a property on a scale), the degree to which something is sophisticated, dignified or intellectual (of conversations, debates, etc.), the level of performance (e.g. of an athlete), level (the context or type of environment within which an interaction, evaulation or explanation is framed)
Nordsee — North Sea.
Nordwesten — northwest.
Notdienst — emergency service.
Notfall — emergency, distress.
Notruf — emergency call.
Nutzung — use.

Ohr — ear (the auditory organ). Also: ear (the external visible part of the organ, the auricle), ear (the ability and proclivity to understand and discern finer details of something, especially sound-based), the overhanging part on the frame of a window or door, a highway interchange which is ear-shaped or ribbon-shaped
Packung — pack, package, packet. Also: a poultice, especially one for cosmetic purposes, a large defeat, battering, the act of packing, a certain method of it
Parkhaus — parking garage, multistorey car park (large building to store cars in).
Parkplatz — parking lot, outdoor car park. Also: parking space, parking spot
Partei — A party in a given one-party system, especially. Also: the NSDAP of Nazi Germany, the SED of East Germany
Patientin — patient (female).
Personalabteilung — department of human resources.
Petersilie — parsley, Petroselinum crispum.

Pfanne — frying pan. Also: short for compounds having -pfanne as their last component, in technical contexts often a metaphor for various implements termed in English mostly socket, as well as in anatomical terminology.
Pflicht — duty.
Phase — phase, stage, period. Also: phase (one of the power-carrying conductors); line conductor
Pilz — mushroom. Also: fungus
Planung — planning.
Prager — Praguer; native or resident of Prague.
Probe — trial. Also: test, rehearsal, sample
Produktion — production.
Präposition — adposition, preposition (broad sense). Also: preposition (narrow sense)
Publikum — audience, those who see, hear, read some work or performance (crowd, viewership, readership etc.). Also: members of the public who frequent some place, institution, establishment
Pyramide — pyramid.
Quadratmeter — square metre.
Quatsch — nonsense. Also: joking, kidding, fun, chat
Realschule — A real school: a secondary school that focuses upon modern science and languages rather than literature, Greek, and Latin.. Also: A type of secondary school.
Redaktion — editing. Also: editorial staff (press, TV etc), newsroom, editorial office
Rede — speech, address.
Regelung — regulation. Also: control (method of governing the performance of an apparatus)
Reifen — tyre; tire. Also: hoop
Reihenfolge — order, sequence.
Reiseführer — tourist guide, tour guide. Also: travel guide
Reklamation — consumer complaint, customer complaint, complaint, claim (a grievance, problem, difficulty, or concern; the act of complaining).
Rente — pension, retirement pay.
Rentner — pensioner (male or of unspecified gender).
Respekt — respect, regard, reverence.
Rind — cow, bull, ox, head of cattle (any bovine animal). Also: beef

Ring — a ring (a circular and hollow object, made of any material.). Also: a ring (small jewelry worn on the finger (usually composed of precious metals)), a ring (a group of people, often involved into criminal and illegal actions), a ring (algebraic structure), a ring (planar geometrical figure), an annulet (ring used as a charge), a ring (a collection of material orbiting a planet), a ring (a place where events such as wrestling and boxing take place), a street that encircles a city where once the old town walls have been; a boulevard.
Rolle — roll, reel, spool. Also: roll, tube, a relatively small wheel on which something is rolled (as on a wheelie bin), role, part, roller, castor, pulley, roll, mangle, wringer, rolling pin, role
Roman — novel.
Rucksack — backpack. Also: rucksack (large, rugged backpack)
Rundgang — walk, tour. Also: rounds, circular gallery
Salon — lounge. Also: salon, saloon, living room
Schatz — treasure. Also: darling, sweetheart, honey
Schauspieler — actor.
Schiedsrichter — judge, referee, umpire.
Schimpfwort — swear word.
Schriftsteller — author, writer.

Schritt — step, footstep (a single instance of walking). Also: walk (slow gait of a horse), crotch (the area between the human legs close to the genitals or comprising them), a pace (any of several obsolete linear measures about as long as a man steps, that is about 70–80 cm), any imaginary measure that is likened in its partitioning to the distance traveled by a leg moving forward in its single footsteps, step, move, stage (the part of a measure in the sense of an action one takes), ellipsis of Schrittgeschwindigkeit
Schulabschluss — graduation (action or process of graduating and receiving a diploma for completing a course of study from an educational institution).
Schulbuch — textbook.
Schuld — debt. Also: fault, guilt, blame, responsibility
Schuljahr — academic year, school year.
Schulkind — schoolchild.
Schulpflicht — compulsory education.
Schultag — schoolday.
Schulzeit — schooldays (period of a person's life when they go to school). Also: teaching time (part of the day or year when children go to school)
Schwein — swine, pig.. Also: A dirty, or contemptible person., luck, good fortune

schwimmen — to swim (to use swimming motions to move in the water). Also: to swim (for pleasure, as a sport, etc.), to swim (a certain distance), to transport by floating, to float, to be floating, to float (somewhere), to move (somewhere) by floating, to be doused or covered in liquid, to be swimming in (money, etc.), to be moving in a blurry or indistinct fashion, to swim (in someone's vision), to lose control (of a situation)
Schönheit — beauty, beautifulness.
Schüssel — bowl, platter. Also: basin, car, (satellite) dish
Sekt — sparkling wine. Also: sack (light-colored dry wine from southern Europe, especially Spain)
Sender — agent noun of senden. Also: sender, broadcaster, transmitter

Sendung — delivery (by a postal service). Also: programme (performance of a show or other broadcast on radio or television), transmission, broadcasting (broadcast of a radio or television programme), motivation, mission
Serie — series (a number of things that follow on one after the other).
Sicherheit — certainty. Also: security, safety
Socke — sock.
Sommerzeit — summertime (the season of summer). Also: summer time; daylight saving time
Song — a song of modern popular music.
Sonntagabend — Sunday evening.
Sorte — a sort, variety, grade of a good produced for sale or consumption. Also: a sort, kind, type of anything, foreign cash; foreign coins and notes
Spielfilm — narrative film, fictional film, fiction film.
Sportart — sport, type of sport.
Sportverein — sports team, sports club.
Sprachkurs — language course.
Sprechstunde — office hours. Also: consultation-hour, surgery
Sprechweise — way of speaking.

Staat — country, state (politically constituted legal person wielding absolute power of a population and territory). Also: state/government, authorities (the entire political and administrative system of such an entity), entourage of a sovereign or prince, stateliness; that which gives the effect of it; fine appearance in clothing and ménage
Standesamt — register office.
Star — starling.
Statistik — statistics. Also: statistics, stats, description of a state, a country
Stau — congestion, stagnancy of water or anything dammed or otherwise backed-up. Also: ellipsis of Verkehrsstau (“traffic jam”), weir

Stein — stone, rock. Also: any stone-like material such as brick or concrete, pit (core of a fruit), ellipsis of Spielstein (“piece, token, tile”), stone (unit of weight), money, bucks, stein (type of beer mug)
Steuer — helm, rudder. Also: steering wheel, the wheel
Stimme — voice (speaking or singing), call of an animal. Also: vote (a person's submission in an election or voting process)

schalten — to switch. Also: to shift gears, to be on the ball (to be quick to understand and react to something sudden and/or unexpected)
schildern — to depict (with words), to narrate, tell vividly. Also: to depict (by illustration), to paint, draw
schimpfen — to tell off, to scold, to yell at. Also: to complain, to grouse, to bitch, to curse, to call, to call oneself

schlagen — to beat; to hit; to knock; to strike; to punch; to hammer; to pound. Also: to beat; to win against; to defeat, to beat; to strike repeatedly; to pound, to beat; to whip; to mix food in a rapid aerating fashion, to chime, to fight, to take, to capture

schlecht — bad, evil, wicked (the opposite of good; immoral). Also: bad (unskilled; of limited ability), bad (unhealthy, unwell), bad (of poor physical appearance), bad, off (spoiled, rotten, overripe), sick, nauseated, dirty (dishonourable)
schließlich — in the end, finally, eventually. Also: after all (because of, for the reason)
schminken — to put on (make-up, lipstick, etc.), to make up one's face. Also: to put on facepaint (e.g. as part of a costume, sometimes also applied to other parts of the body)
schmücken — to adorn, to decorate.
schnell — quickly. Also: again; quick; used after a question to imply that one ought to know the answer
Schwedisch — Swedish (language).
schwer — heavy, weighty. Also: difficult, hard, serious, indigestible
schwierig — difficult, hard, challenging, tough. Also: difficult, prickly
selbstbewusst — self-confident, self-assured. Also: self-conscious, self-aware
senden — to broadcast; to transmit. Also: to send
sichern — to secure. Also: to guarantee, to assure
sinnvoll — meaningful. Also: sensible
sortieren — to sort.
sowieso — anyway, anyhow, in any case.
sozial — social.

sparen — to save up; to accumulate, to put aside (for a specific purpose). Also: to save, to conserve (for example energy), to save, be able to save (as unnecessary or useless), keep something to oneself, to save, to spare (from effort, an unpleasant or unnecessary task)
speziell — special. Also: particular, specific
spontan — spontaneous. Also: ad hoc
stattfinden — to take place, to happen.
Stecken — stick.
stehlen — to steal. Also: to skulk, to move secretly

stoßen — to push; to shove; to thrust. Also: to bump; to knock; to strike; to hurt, to take exception [with an (+ dative) ‘to something’], to jolt; to kick; to thrust, to bump, to knock [with an or gegen (+ accusative) ‘into/against something’], to come across, to happen upon, to stumble [with auf (+ accusative) ‘across/upon something’]
streichen — to stroke. Also: to cancel; to discard; to delete; to strike out; to cross out, to spread; to rub; to apply, to paint
streiten — to argue; to fight; to quarrel.
streng — strict, rigorous. Also: severe, intense, pungent, strong and unpleasant
streuen — to strew, to scatter, to sprinkle. Also: to spread
stundenlang — lasting for hours.
stören — to disturb, to interfere, to bother.

stürzen — to fall down, to drop, to tumble. Also: to dash, to rush, to sprint to something, to drop off steeply, to throw, to hurl, to upturn, to overthrow, to oust, to dethrone, to plunge, to dive, to throw oneself [with auf (+ accusative) ‘into something’]
tabellarisch — tabular.
tanken — to fuel, to refuel (fill a fuel tank of a vehicle). Also: to drink a lot of alcohol, to move forcefully against resistance
tariflich — tariff. Also: according to the agreed union rate
teilnehmen — to participate (in).
telefonisch — telephonic. Also: contactable by telephone
testen — to test (by experiment or scientific method).
teuer — expensive, dear (high in price). Also: dear (precious, valued)
treiben — to drive (e.g. livestock); to propel; to force. Also: to put forth; to produce; to sprout, to urge, to do, to get up to, to drift; to float about, to sprout
trocknen — to dry (to become dry, cease being wet). Also: to dry (to make dry or let become dry)
typisch — typical.
umgekehrt — reverse.
umweltbewusst — environmentally-friendly.
unabhängig — independent.
ungewöhnlich — uncommon, unusual, unordinary.
unglaublich — unbelievable, incredible.
unglücklich — unhappy, unjoyous, unjoyful, misfortunate, miserable. Also: unfortunate, unlucky, inauspicious, unauspicious, untoward, hapless
unhöflich — rude. Also: uncivil
unsicher — uncertain. Also: unsafe, insecure
Unter — jack, knave (playing card).
unterhalten — to maintain. Also: to entertain, to converse

unterstützen — to support, to assist, to help, to aid. Also: to endorse, to give support, to show support, to back up, to promote, to encourage, to sponsor, to support, to sustain, to support, to uphold, to maintain, to enhance, to juice
unzufrieden — discontent, dissatisfied.
ursprünglich — original, initial, first. Also: original, authentic, genuine, unadulterated
vegetarisch — vegetarian.
verantwortlich — responsible. Also: answerable
verbessern — to improve (to make better), to enhance. Also: to improve (to become better), to correct
verbinden — to join, to combine, to connect, to interlink. Also: to bandage, to put through
verbrauchen — to consume, to use up.

verbrennen — to burn (to cause to be consumed by fire or flames), to incinerate, to combust. Also: to burn oneself, to suffer a burning, to scorch, to burn (to hurt the mouth by an overdose of spices), to be burned
vereinbaren — to agree upon. Also: to arrange, to reconcile
vergeben — to forgive. Also: to assign; to allocate; to give (a job); to give or set (a task); to award (a contract), to give away
vergleichen — to compare.
vergrößern — to enlarge, to amplify, to increase. Also: to become larger
verhindern — to prevent, to inhibit, to keep (something) from happening..
Verlangen — desire, yearning, longing. Also: request, wish, demand
verlaufen — to get lost, stray. Also: to disperse, scatter, to run (extend in a specific direction), to go, proceed (well, poorly, etc.), to peter out, to melt
verlieben — to fall in love.
verlängern — to lengthen. Also: to prolong, to extend
verrücken — to shift, to move by pushing.
versammeln — to gather, to assemble (a group of people for an event, meeting etc.). Also: to gather (at a location)
verschicken — to send out. Also: to send off
verstecken — to conceal. Also: to hide (oneself)

verstellen — to adjust, alter, misalign. Also: to displace, shift, to put back in the wrong place, to block (an entrance, path etc.), to disguise (one's voice etc.), to dissemble, dissimulate, pretend, sham
verwenden — to use, to employ, to apply. Also: to make expenses applied on a material object, to intercede, to turn away
verändern — to alter, to change. Also: to change, to change job
viermal — four times. Also: quadruply
voneinander — from / of one another, from / of each other.
vorbereiten — to prepare. Also: to prepare, to get ready
vorn — in front.
vorschlagen — to propose, to suggest.
vorsichtig — deliberate (not hasty or sudden); careful, cautious, wary, circumspect.
vorwärts — forwards, forward; onwards, onward.
völlig — complete, total, utter.
wachsen — to grow. Also: to grow, to increase
wahrscheinlich — likely, probable, plausible.
weggehen — to go away, leave, scram.
wegwerfen — to throw away, to discard.
wehen — to blow (wind, a storm, etc.). Also: to flutter, to wave (in the wind); to fly (of a flag)
weich — soft.
weitergehen — to proceed, progress, continue. Also: to keep moving
weiterhin — still. Also: also, going further, farther, further
weltbekannt — world-famous.
weniger — less. Also: minus
wenigstens — at least.
widersprechen — to object; to disagree. Also: to contradict, to be contradictory
wiegen — to weigh; to be of a certain weight. Also: to weigh; to measure the weight of
wieso — why. Also: how so

wirken — to function, to be effective, to work. Also: to appear, to seem, to have an effect, to perform (some action), to achieve (some effect), to work (cause to happen), to cast (a spell), to work or be active in a certain field or activity, to work, to take effect, to be effective, to knit (with yarn), to brocade, to knead
wunderbar — wonderful.
wundern — to surprise, to astonish (of sights, behaviors, outcomes). Also: to be surprised, to wonder, to consider something strange, to be confused
wundervoll — wonderful.
wöchentlich — weekly, every week. Also: weekly, once every week
zahlreich — numerous.
Zehnt — tithe (a tenthpart of the increase).

ziehen — to pull; to drag. Also: to draw (e.g. a weapon); to extract; to puff, to draw (a conclusion, lesson, etc.), to be drafty; there to be a draft (current of air), to move; to migrate, to roam; to head, to stretch; to warp, pull
zumindest — at least.
zurückbringen — to bring back, recall, restore.
zurückgehen — to go back, return (to a place). Also: to decline, abate
zurückkehren — to return.
zustimmen — to agree. Also: to agree with, to be in accord with

zwar — Signals a following contrary clause, which is usually introduced with aber (“but”), stressing that the speaker is aware of the contradiction, but that it does not invalidate the statement.. Also: namely, Intensifies a request or command.
zweitens — secondly.
zünden — to kindle. Also: to ignite a fire or any comparable combustion
Ärger — annoyance, anger. Also: trouble, an unpleasant or dangerous situation
Öffnungszeit — opening hours.
Überraschung — surprise.
Überstunde — hour of overtime. Also: overtime
ändern — to change, to alter. Also: to change, to vary
ärgern — to annoy, to make angry. Also: to be annoyed, to be angry, to get annoyed, to get angry
ökologisch — ecologic, ecological. Also: environmental
österreichisch — Austrian.
überhaupt — overall, in general. Also: at all; anyway, actually, even
überprüfen — to check, to verify.
übersetzen — to translate, to interpret. Also: to compile, to take too much compensation from, to plant too much on
übrigens — apart from that, else, for the rest, for everything else. Also: by the way

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
